proposalOpportunities.detail: Partner with RAFT as co-PI or collaborating institution on national and international calls.

supportOptions.title: Fund a Research Theme
supportOptions.summary: Back a focused programme — urban flooding, early warning or AI-for-climate.

supportOptions.title: Sponsor a Product
supportOptions.summary: Help take SnapFlood, UFIS or VarshaAnalytics from research to wider deployment.

supportOptions.summary: Contribute sensors, drones, data or compute that accelerate the work.

faqs.a: RAFT is Rainfall-runoff Analysis, Modelling and Forecasting Tools — a hydroinformatics research group and innovation lab in the Department of Civil Engineering at IIT Hyderabad.
The Urban Flood Information System (UFIS) is RAFT’s flagship effort: it brings rainfall, hydrologic and flood-flow information together into forecasts and decision-support for cities and agencies.
RAFT is hosted at the Department of Civil Engineering, IIT Hyderabad.

faqs.q: How can my institution collaborate with RAFT?
faqs.a: Reach out via the contact page with a short note on your area of interest. We collaborate on funded research, joint proposals, student exchange and operational pilots.

faqs.q: Do you work with city and state agencies?
faqs.a: Yes. A core goal of UFIS is to deliver flood-relevant information to decision-makers, so we actively seek partnerships with municipal and state agencies.

faqs.q: Can industry partners work with RAFT?
faqs.a: Yes — through sponsored research, data partnerships or CSR-funded innovation. Get in touch and we’ll shape an engagement that fits.
Yes — RAFT welcomes PhD, Master’s and project students. Doctoral admissions are processed through IIT Hyderabad; for project and internship roles, get in touch directly.
Doctoral admissions run through IIT Hyderabad’s standard process. If your research interests align with RAFT, mention it when you apply and feel free to reach out beforehand.

faqs.q: How can we support RAFT’s work?
faqs.a: Support ranges from funding a research theme and sponsoring a product to co-funding a pilot or contributing equipment, data and compute. Tell us your goals and we’ll tailor an arrangement.

faqs.q: Can we sponsor a specific product or pilot?
faqs.a: Yes — you can sponsor a product such as SnapFlood or VarshaAnalytics, or co-fund a city or basin pilot shaped around real operational needs.

SnapFlood is a free citizen-science app that lets anyone capture and upload geotagged flood photos and videos, building a real-time flood-observation archive that feeds research, modelling and faster municipal response. It’s available on Web, Android and iOS.
Yes — SnapFlood is a free, lightweight app and needs no specialist hardware.
VarshaAnalytics is RAFT’s rainfall-analytics product — it brings station, radar, satellite and model rainfall together into a single, easy-to-read view for communities and decision-makers.
